title: Power saving signal configurations for connected discontinuous reception
abstract: A method, system and apparatus are disclosed. According to one or more embodiments, a method implemented at a wireless device is provided. The wireless device is configured with a connected discontinuous reception, C-DRX, mode defining an active mode and a non-active mode. The method comprises receiving an indication of a go-to-sleep, GTS, signal when in non-active mode, the GTS signal indicating to the wireless device to stay in non-active mode during an upcoming active mode time defined by the wireless device's C-DRX configuration.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method implemented at a wireless device configured with a connected discontinuous reception, C-DRX, mode defining an active mode and a non-active mode, the method comprising: receiving an indication of a go-to-sleep, GTS, signal when in non-active mode, the GTS signal indicating to the wireless device to stay in non-active mode during an upcoming active mode time defined by the wireless deviceâs C-DRX configuration. 2. The method of claim 1 , the method further comprising operating according to the indication of the GTS signal by staying in non-active mode during the upcoming active mode time. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the method comprises receiving the indication of the GTS signal over a physical downlink control channel, PDCCH. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the method comprises receiving the indication of the GTS signal in downlink control information, DCI. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the DCI comprises an indication of the GTS signal. 6. The method of any preceding claim, wherein the method comprises receiving the indication of the GTS signal in at least one monitored configured control resource set, CORESET. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the at least one CORESET is a CORESET specific to the GTS signal. 8 The method of claim 6, wherein the at least one CORESET is shared for other PDCCH monitoring. 9. A wireless device configured with a connected discontinuous reception, C-DRX, mode defining a an active mode and a non-active mode, the wireless device configured to: receive an indication of a go-to-sleep, GTS, signal when in non-active mode, the GTS signal indicating to the wireless device to stay in non-active mode during an upcoming active mode time defined by the wireless deviceâs C-DRX configuration. 10. The wireless device of claim 9, wherein the wireless device is further configured to operate according to the indication of the GTS signal by staying in non-active mode during the upcoming active mode time. 11. The wireless device of claim 9 or 10, wherein the wireless device is configured to receive the indication of the GTS signal over a physical downlink control channel, PDCCH. 12. The wireless device of claim 11, wherein the wireless device is configured to receive the indication of the GTS signal in downlink control information, DCI. 13. The wireless device of 12, wherein the DCI comprises an indication of the GTS signal. 14. The wireless device of any of claims 9 to 13, wherein the wireless device is configured to receive the indication of the GTS signal in at least one monitored configured control resource set, CORESET. 15. The wireless device of claim 14, wherein the at least one CORESET is a CORESET specific to the GTS signal. 16. The wireless device of claim 14, wherein the at least one CORESET is shared for other PDCCH monitoring. 17. The method of claim 17, wherein the method comprises indicating the GTS signal over a physical downlink control channel, PDCCH. 19. The method of claim 17 or 18, wherein the method comprises indicating the GTS signal in downlink control information, DCI. 20. The method of any of claims 17 to 20, wherein the method comprises indicating the GTS signal in at least one configured control resource set, CORESET. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the at least one CORESET is a CORESET specific to the GTS signal. 23. The method of claim 21, wherein the at least one CORESET is shared for other PDCCH monitoring. 24. A network node configured to communicate with a wireless device configured with a connected discontinuous reception, C-DRX, operation mode defining an active mode and a non-active mode, the network node configured to: indicate a go-to-sleep, GTS, signal to the wireless device when the wireless device is in non-active mode, the GTS signal indicating to the wireless device to stay in non-active mode during an upcoming active mode time defined by the wireless deviceâs C-DRX configuration. 25. The network node of claim 25, wherein the network node is configured to indicate the GTS signal in downlink control information, DCI. 27. The network node of claim 26, wherein the DCI comprises an indication of the GTS signal. 28. The network node of any of claims 24 to 27, wherein the network node is configured to indicate the GTS signal in at least one configured control resource set, CORESET. 29. The network node of claim 28, wherein the at least one CORESET is a CORESET specific to the GTS signal. 30. The method of claim 31, wherein the method further comprises monitoring the CORESET for an indication of the power-saving signal. 33. The method of claim 31 or 32, wherein the power saving signal is a go-to-sleep, GTS, signal indicating to the wireless device to stay outside active mode during an upcoming active mode time defined by the wireless deviceâs C-DRX configuration. 34. The method of claim 31 or 32, wherein the power saving signal is a wake-up signal, WUS, indicating to the wireless device to transition to active mode from non-active mode. 35. The method of any of claims 31 to 34, wherein the at least one CORESET is a CORESET specific to the power-saving signal. 36. The method of any of claims 31 to 34, wherein the at least one CORESET is shared for other physical downlink channel, PDCCH, monitoring. 37. A wireless device configured with a connected discontinuous reception, C-DRX, mode defining an active mode and a non-active mode, the wireless device configured to: 

 receive an assignment of at least one control resource set, CORESET, associated with at least one search space to monitor for an indication of a power-saving signal outside active mode. 38. The wireless device of claim 37 or 38, wherein the power saving signal is a go-to-sleep, GTS, signal indicating to the wireless device to stay outside active mode in an upcoming active mode time defined by the wireless deviceâs C-DRX configuration. 40. The wireless device of claim 37 or 38, wherein the power saving signal is a wake-up signal, WUS, indicating to the wireless device to transition to active mode from non-active mode. 41. The wireless device of any of claims 37 to 40, wherein the at least one CORESET is a CORESET specific to the power-saving signal. 42. The method of claim 43, wherein the power saving signal is a go-to-sleep, GTS, signal indicating to the wireless device to stay outside active mode during an upcoming active mode time defined by the wireless deviceâs C-DRX configuration. 45. The method of claim 43, wherein the power saving signal is a wake-up signal, WUS, indicating to the wireless device to transition to active mode from non-active mode. 46. The method of any of claims 43 to 45, wherein the at least one CORESET is a CORESET specific to the power-saving signal. 47. A network node configured to communicate with a wireless device configured with a connected discontinuous reception, C-DRX, mode defining an active mode and a non-active mode, the network node configured to: assign to the wireless device at least one control resource set, CORESET, associated with at least one search space to monitor for an indication of a power-saving signal outside active mode. 49. The network node of claim 48, wherein the power saving signal is a wake-up signal, WUS, indicating to the wireless device to transition to active mode from non-active mode. 51. The network node of any of claims 48 to 50, wherein the at least one CORESET is a CORESET specific to the power-saving signal. 52. The network node of any of claims 48 to 50, wherein the at least one CORESET is shared for other physical downlink channel, PDCCH, monitoring. 53. A method implemented at a wireless device configured with a connected discontinuous reception, C-DRX, mode defining an active mode and a non-active mode, the method comprising: receiving an indication of a go-to-sleep, GTS, signal over a physical downlink control channel, PDCCH, when in active mode during an active mode time defined by the wireless deviceâs C-DRX configuration, the GTS signal indicating to the wireless device to transition to non-active mode during the active mode time. 54. The method of claim 53, further comprising transitioning from the active mode to the non active mode during the active mode time. 55. The method of any of claims 53 to 55, further comprising: receiving an indication of a wake-up signal, WUS, when in non-active mode prior to the active mode time indicating to the wireless device to transition to active mode. 57. The method of claim 56, further comprising transitioning from the non-active mode to the active mode. 58. A wireless device configured with a connected discontinuous reception, C-DRX, mode defining an active mode and a non-active mode, the wireless device configured to: receive an indication of a go-to-sleep, GTS, signal over a physical downlink control channel, PDCCH, when in active mode during an active mode time defined by the wireless deviceâs C-DRX configuration, the GTS signal indicating to the wireless device to transition to non-active mode during the active mode time. 59. The wireless device of claim 58, wherein the wireless device is further configured to transition to the non-active mode during the active mode time in response to receiving the GTS signal. 60. The wireless device of claim 58 or 59, wherein the wireless device is configured to receive the indication of the GTS signal in downlink control information, DCI. 61. The wireless device of any of claims 58 to 60, the device further configured to: receive an indication of a wake-up signal, WUS, when in non-active mode prior to the active mode time indicating to the wireless device to transition to active mode. 62. The wireless device of claim 61, further configured to transition from the non-active mode to the active mode in response to receiving the WUS. 63. A method implemented at a network node configured to communicate with a wireless device configured with a connected discontinuous reception, C-DRX, mode defining an active mode and a non-active mode, the method comprising: indicating a go-to-sleep, GTS, signal over a physical downlink control channel, PDCCH, to the wireless device when the wireless device is in an active mode during an active mode time defined by the 

 deviceâs C-DRX configuration, the GTS signal indicating to the wireless device to transition to non-active mode during the active mode time. 64. The method of claim 63, further comprising sending an indication of a wake-up signal, WUS, when the wireless device is in non-active mode prior to the active mode time, the WUS indicating to the wireless device to transition to active mode. 65. A network node configured to communicate with a wireless device configured with a connected discontinuous reception, C-DRX, mode defining an active mode and a non-active mode, the network node configured to: indicate a go-to-sleep, GTS, signal over a physical downlink control channel, PDCCH, to the wireless device when the wireless device is in an active mode during an active mode time defined by the deviceâs C-DRX configuration, the GTS signal indicating to the wireless device to transition to non-active mode during the active mode time. 66. The network node of claim 65, further configured to send an indication of a wake-up signal, WUS, when the wireless device is in non-active mode prior to the active mode time, the WUS indicating to the wireless device to transition to active mode.
